recalls.Description: IKEA is recalling its 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic presses, which are designed for crushing garlic cloves. These garlic presses feature a black rubber handle and a zinc-coated garlic chamber. Only those models displaying the IKEA logo on the upper part of the handle are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: IKEA Recalls VÄRDEFULL Garlic Presses Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact IKEA at 888-966-4532 from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls. You can also access their website at www.ikea.com/us/en and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL Garlic Press
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 43,830 (in addition, about 10,700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic press
Recalled IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic press logo identification

Injuries.Name: IKEA has received a total of 10 incident reports globally, including three reports of lacerations and finger splinters. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at IKEA stores nationwide and online at IKEA.com from March 2025 through May 2025 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: IKEA US Retail LLC, of Woodbridge, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or ingestion from detached metal pieces

Remedies.Name: Stop using the garlic press and return it to any IKEA store for a full refund. If returning is not possible, contact IKEA for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. No proof of purchase is needed.

recalls.metaDescription: IKEA recalls VÄRDEFULL garlic presses due to safety hazards. Learn how to return for a full refund.

recalls.Description: Greater Goods is recalling its Digital Kitchen Scales due to safety concerns related to coin batteries. The affected models include 0480, 0455, 0456, 0458, 0473, 0479, 0481, 0747, 0748, 0749, 0751, 0752, and 0754. These scales, available in various colors such as gray, black, blue, green, pink, red, silver, and white, feature the Greater Goods logo on the front panel and come with a pre-installed lithium CR3032 battery, along with a spare battery in the packaging. The design allows easy access to the battery, posing a risk to children.
recalls.Title: Greater Goods Recalls Digital Kitchen Scales Due to Ingestion Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to contact Greater Goods at 866-991-0397 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recall@greatergoods.com. Additional information can be found on their website at www.greatergoods.com by clicking the recall banner at the top of the page or visiting https://shop.greatergoods.com/pages/replaceyourbatterydoor.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gray Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Black Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Cherry Red Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Black Glass Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Silver Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Blue Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Mint Green Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Pink Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled White Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Green Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale Packaging – Back (Sticker color will match Scale color)
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale Packaging – Front (Sticker color will match Scale color
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale without battery door - Bottom
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale - Bottom
Recalled battery door and updated battery door

Retailers.Name: Available at Alexandra's Kitchen in Niskayuna, New York, as well as online through Amazon, Walmart, and Greater Goods from May 2023 to September 2024 for about $10.

Importers.Name: Greater Goods LLC, of Saint Louis, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible coin battery and non-child resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Consumers should remove the battery immediately and contact Greater Goods for a free recall kit, which includes a secure replacement battery door, necessary labeling, and an updated manual.

recalls.metaDescription: Greater Goods recalls Digital Kitchen Scales due to ingestion risks from coin batteries. Contact for a free recall kit and safety updates.

recalls.Description: QVC is recalling Temp-tations Oven Gloves due to a potential burn hazard. These gloves, available in various colors such as blue, yellow, red, and floral patterns, were sold individually, in pairs, or as part of sets that included drying mats or trivets. The gloves feature model numbers K51459, K76398, K47973, K48879, K85322, K96004, K92603, K308719, K309220, K309388, and K309516, and are made from cotton and elastane in both small and large sizes. A label reading 'Temp-tations by Tara' is stitched inside the gloves.
recalls.Title: QVC Recalls Over One Million Temp-tations Oven Gloves Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach QVC at 888-770-7119 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email ovengloves@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/ovengloves or www.qvc.com and select 'Product Recall Info' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Temp-tations Oven Gloves
Recalled Temp-tations Oven Gloves (various colors)

Injuries.Name: QVC has received 162 reports of insufficient heat protection including 92 reports of minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available on QVC.com, through QVC televised shows, and QVC digital shopping platforms from August 2018 to August 2024, priced between $4 and $13 per pair, or $14 to $26 for bundled sets.

Importers.Name: QVC Inc., of West Chester, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Insufficient heat protection leading to burn risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gloves and contact QVC for a refund

recalls.metaDescription: QVC recalls Temp-tations Oven Gloves due to burn hazards. Learn how to get a refund and stay safe in the kitchen.

recalls.Description: Walmart has issued a recall for the Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers, identified by model number MS14100094536S1. These cordless, rechargeable choppers feature a green lid, a white body, and a clear plastic bowl. The blade attachment consists of three curved blades mounted on a white plastic spindle, and the model number can be found on a label on the bottom of the bowl.
recalls.Title: Walmart Recalls Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled choppers immediately and contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT any day, or visit https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper container with blade and lid
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled

Injuries.Name: Walmart has received five reports of lacerations, two of which required medical attention, including stitches. Injuries occurred during assembly of the product or during cleaning/handling of the product.

Manufacturers.Name: Shuangma Plastic Manufacturing Inc., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations nationwide and online at Walmart.com from August 2022 to October 2023, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Walmart Stores Inc., of Bentonville, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Unexpected blade operation during assembly or when not enclosed, leading to laceration risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chopper and contact Walmart for a full refund; returns can be made at any Walmart store.

recalls.metaDescription: Walmart recalls Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers due to a laceration hazard. Stop using immediately and contact Walmart for a refund.
